originalTitle: Millions listen to Ethiopian star's song taking swipe at government
title: Our Super Singer Asks Us All to Be Friends!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Welcome, young friends! Your friendly news reporter from Africa is here. We have a super exciting story to share with you today from a beautiful country called Ethiopia. Get ready to learn about a very special song!

Meet Our Super Singer!

Imagine your favorite singer! The one who makes you want to dance and sing along. In Ethiopia, there is a super-duper famous singer named Teddy Afro. Lots and lots of people love his music. When Teddy Afro sings, people all over Ethiopia listen. His songs are full of wonderful messages. He is like a superstar! He sings with all his heart. People play his music in their homes, in their cars, and even in shops. Everyone knows his face and his amazing voice. He helps people feel happy and think about important things. Did you know? Music is like a magic spell! It can make you feel happy, sad, or even thoughtful. Teddy Afro's music is very powerful!

A Special New Song for Everyone

Teddy Afro has a brand new song! And guess what it's about? It's about being friends and helping each other. He sings that everyone in Ethiopia should come together, like a big, happy family. He wants everyone to work side-by-side. He thinks that if people are united, they can make their country even stronger and happier. His song tells a story of how special it is when we all get along. This song is very popular! Millions of people are listening to it. Imagine millions of people listening to the same song, at the same time! That's how many people love Teddy Afro and his music. Did you know? Ethiopia is a very big country in Africa! It has tall mountains, wide rivers, and lots of different kinds of people who live there. It's famous for coffee too!

What is 'Unity'?

Teddy Afro's song talks about 'unity'. What does that big word mean? It means being together. It means being friends and helping each other, even if you are different. Think about your classroom. When everyone plays nicely, shares toys, and helps clean up, that's unity! If one friend is sad, others help. If you are building a big tower with blocks, you all work together. That's unity! It makes everything better and more fun. When people have unity, they don't argue much. They smile more. They share their ideas and listen to each other. It's like everyone is part of the same team. Did you know? Even small ants work together in unity to build their homes and find food! They are great team players!

Being Friends Makes Us Strong

Teddy Afro sings that when people are united, they are strong. Imagine trying to push a very big box all by yourself. It's hard! But if all your friends help you push, it becomes easy. That's what unity does for a country. When everyone helps each other, the country becomes strong. Everyone feels safe and happy. There are more smiles and more kindness. His song helps people remember this important idea. He wants everyone to hold hands, metaphorically, and walk forward together. He wants all people to be kind to one another. It's a wonderful message for grown-ups and for kids too! We can all learn to be more friendly and helpful every day. Did you know? Ethiopia is one of the oldest countries in the world! It has a very long history with many kings and queens.

Our Beautiful Home: Ethiopia

Ethiopia is a land of beautiful sights and sounds. It has yummy food and exciting festivals. Teddy Afro wants all the people in this amazing country to live in peace and friendship. He wants everyone to remember that they are all part of the same big family. A family called Ethiopia. He wants them to share joy and help each other when things are tough. His song is like a gentle reminder, saying: "Let's be good to each other. Let's make our home the best place it can be!" Did you know? Ethiopia is home to some unique animals like the Gelada Baboon, which you can't find anywhere else in the world! What a special place!

Singing for a Happy Future

So, Teddy Afro, our big singer, has given a gift to his country. A song that asks everyone to be friends. A song that asks for unity. It's a song that makes people think about how important it is to get along. And it shows that even a song can make a big difference in the world! So, let's all try to be like Teddy Afro. Let's sing our own songs of friendship and unity in our schools, our homes, and our playgrounds. Because when we are all friends, the world is a happier place! Let's all listen to Teddy Afro's message and spread kindness. Yay for friendship and unity!
summary: Famous Ethiopian singer Teddy Afro has released a popular new song asking everyone in his country to be united and friendly, working together like a big family.
whyMatters: This is important because when people in a country are friends and help each other, it makes everyone happier and the country stronger, just like when you play nicely with your friends at school.

title: DR Congo accepts first set of deportees from the US
content: The Congolese government stresses those expelled from the US are only in the country temporarily.

content: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

originalTitle: Trump claims on Iranian concessions trigger questions, rejections in Tehran
title: Big Ships and a Busy Watery Road!
content:
Hello, Super Reporters!

Hello, amazing friends! Welcome to our special news report. We're going to talk about something happening far, far away, across the big blue ocean. It's about a very special watery road that many big ships use every single day. Sounds exciting, right? Let's find out more!

What is the Strait of Hormuz?

Imagine your street, but instead of cars and bicycles, there are giant ships! And instead of tarmac, it's all water. Well, there's a place like that called the Strait of Hormuz. Say it with me: Hor-muz. It's not a street, it's a very narrow part of the ocean, like a very busy shortcut. This shortcut connects two big parts of the sea. **Did you know?** A "strait" is like a water bridge, a narrow path of water that joins two bigger pieces of water! It's like a magical river that never ends, connecting vast oceans.

Why is this Watery Road So Important?

This watery road is super important because lots and lots of big ships travel through it. What do these ships carry? All sorts of things! They carry oil, which helps cars go, makes electricity, and helps cook food. They also carry other important things like food, clothes, toys, and even parts for making computers and phones! Imagine if all the trucks on your road stopped moving. It would be a problem, right? The Strait of Hormuz is like that, but for super-sized ships that carry things for people all over the world. Many countries need the oil and other things that pass through this special watery road. It helps the world keep moving! **Did you know?** Some ships are so big, they are longer than three football fields put together! They can carry enough oil to fill millions of cars!

What Does 'Restricted' Mean?

Now, sometimes, people talk about this watery road being "restricted." What does "restricted" mean? It's like when your mom or dad tells you, "Don't run in the house," or "Stay on the path." It means there are rules or things that make it harder to pass through. So, if the Strait of Hormuz is restricted, it means it might be harder or slower for the big ships to go through. Maybe they have to go slower, or take a different path, or there are new rules. It's not like the road is closed completely, but it makes things a bit tricky. When a road is restricted, it can take longer for things to get from one place to another. This can make things a little more difficult for everyone.

Who is Talking About This?

In the country near the Strait of Hormuz, some grown-ups have said that it might be restricted. These are important people who work for the government there. They are part of a group called the IRGC, but that's a very long name for us today! For now, just know that some grown-ups in that area made an announcement. When grown-ups make announcements like this, it's usually because they want to show they are serious about something. Other grown-ups in different countries pay close attention to these announcements. They want to make sure everyone can still get their important things, like oil, safely. It's like when grown-ups have a disagreement, and they talk loudly about rules. Everyone listens to see what will happen next.

Why Does This Matter to Us in Africa?

You might be thinking, "This is happening so far away! Why should I care?" That's a great question! Even though the Strait of Hormuz is not in Africa, the world is all connected, like a big puzzle. Many of the big ships that carry oil and other goods might travel paths that eventually affect our continent. If it becomes harder for ships to move through that important watery road, it can make things more expensive for everyone, everywhere. For example, if oil takes longer to get to places, the price of fuel for our cars and buses here in Africa might go up a little bit. Also, when countries far away have disagreements, it can make everyone feel a bit worried. But don't worry too much! Grown-ups all over the world are always trying to find ways to make peace and keep things running smoothly. Our leaders in Africa are also watching and hoping for everything to be peaceful. **Did you know?** The ocean currents and trade routes connect almost every country in the world! So, something happening far away can sometimes touch us too, like ripples in a pond.

Grown-ups Are Working Hard!

It's important to remember that grown-ups, like our leaders and smart people from different countries, are talking to each other. They want to make sure everyone can use the watery road safely and fairly. They are trying to solve any problems and find the best ways forward. Just like when you have a problem with a friend, talking helps to make things better. Grown-ups are doing a lot of talking to make sure ships can keep moving and everyone gets what they need. They want to keep the world a safe and happy place for all of us. We hope that everyone will be kind and work together so that the Strait of Hormuz, and all other important paths, stay open and safe for everyone to use.

Let's Learn About Ships!

Let's think about how amazing ships are! They are like giant floating cities, carrying so much cargo. Some ships even have huge engines that make them travel across vast oceans for weeks! Imagine living on a ship for that long! **Did you know?** Ships have special names, just like us! And they have a captain, who is like the super-boss of the ship, making sure everyone is safe and the ship gets where it needs to go. Many ships carry colourful containers, like big LEGO bricks, stacked high! These containers hold all sorts of things, from your favourite shoes to parts for your phone.

Keeping the World Moving

So, while this news might sound a little bit complicated, remember the main idea: there's a very important watery road for big ships, and sometimes there are rules that make it a bit harder to use. But lots of smart and kind grown-ups are working together to keep everything moving safely and peacefully for everyone around the world. We can all hope for cooperation and peace!
summary: An important watery road called the Strait of Hormuz, used by big ships carrying oil and other goods, might have new rules that make it harder to pass through, and grown-ups are working to keep things safe and peaceful.
whyMatters: This matters because if it's harder for big ships to move, things like oil and other goods could take longer to reach places, possibly making them a little more expensive, and it's always good for countries to work together peacefully.

originalTitle: Slavery reparations are just, but who exactly owes whom?
title: Slavery reparations are just, but who exactly owes whom?
content: Some African elites benefited from slave trade and colonisation. This must be taken into account in reparation debates.
